40 CFR Part 355 Appendix A (Cornell Law) - Toluene (CAS 108-88-3) is NOT listed as an EPCRA 302 EHS. The list contains isomers like Toluene 2,4-Diisocyanate (CAS 584-84-9) and Toluene 2,6-Diisocyanate (CAS 620-14-4), but not plain Toluene. EPA regulations state that 'toluene is not listed as a SARA Section 302 Extremely Hazardous Substance'.
40 CFR Part 355 Appendix A (Cornell Law) - Since Toluene is not an EPCRA 302 EHS, it has no TPQ under EPCRA Section 302

40 CFR Part 355 Appendix A (Cornell Law) - Acetone (CAS 67-64-1) is NOT listed as an EPCRA 302 EHS. The list contains Acetone Cyanohydrin (CAS 75-86-5), which is a different chemical with a TPQ of 1,000 lbs, but plain Acetone is not listed as an EHS.
40 CFR Part 355 Appendix A (Cornell Law) - Since plain Acetone (CAS 67-64-1) is not an EPCRA 302 EHS, it has no TPQ under EPCRA Section 302

privileged_info_summary: The task requires screening five chemicals via CAMEO Chemicals to determine EPCRA 302 EHS status. All ground truth values have been verified against official regulatory sources (40 CFR Part 355 Appendix A). Phosgene (CAS 75-44-5) is an EHS with TPQ of 10 lbs. Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5) is an EHS with TPQ of 100 lbs. Sulfuric Acid (CAS 7664-93-9) is an EHS with TPQ of 1,000 lbs. Toluene (CAS 108-88-3) is NOT an EPCRA 302 EHS (note: isomers like Toluene 2,4-Diisocyanate are listed separately with different CAS numbers). Acetone (CAS 67-64-1) is NOT an EPCRA 302 EHS (note: Acetone Cyanohydrin, CAS 75-86-5, is a different chemical that is listed as an EHS with TPQ of 1,000 lbs). The data comes from CAMEO Chemicals, a real NOAA database, making this a real-world task with authentic regulatory data.
